records.ety: [L. extinguere, exstinguere; ex out + stinguere to quench. See Distinguish, Finish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Extinguished(ks*t"gwsht); p pr. & vb. n. Extinguishing.]

defs: To quench; to put out, as a light or fire; to stifle; to cause to die out; to put an end to; to destroy; as, to extinguish a flame, or life, or love, or hope, a pretense or a right.
quotes: A light which the fierce winds have no power to extinguish.
This extinguishes my right to the reversion.

defs: To obscure; to eclipse, as by superior splendor.
